© 2023. All rights reserved.

Hi! I'm Grant.

I’m interested in the intrinsic structures present within extensive, high-dimensional biomedical datasets, and the circumstances under which these structures can be recovered, differentiated, and interpretated. I am lucky to be advised by Dr. Dan Howrigan, Dr. Wei Zhou, and Daniel Lazarev in Dr. Ben Neale’s Lab of the Broad Institute/ATGU, where I assist in the design and implementation of novel algorithms to uncover the genetic basis of complex diseases.

I completed my undergraduate training in maths and computer science at Northeastern University in the Khoury College of Computer Sciences. Previously, I worked on topics in applied computer vision, self-supervised learning, and computational pathology, under the various supervisions of Dr. Ennio Mingolla at Northeastern University and Evan Liu and Dr. Sertan Kaya at Genentech.

I have been extremely fortunate to have great mentors. If you think that you would benefit from chatting with me about seeking work or research opportunities, my experiences at various instiutions, etc., please do not hesitate to reach out.

Contact Me

Please reach out by email at gchau<at>broadinstitute<dot>org, or on my socials linked on the side.

I am applying to graduate programs to begin a PhD in Fall 2024. I would love to talk with potential advisors about mutual research interests!
